methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kraken Robotics is a publicly listed company in the Defense & Security sector. The broader peer universe comprises 21 same-sector companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. However, the peer set lacks sufficient homogeneity for reliable valuation triangulation.

The Peer-Median method is not applicable to this analysis. A rigorous peer-median approach requires a minimum of three comparable companies filtered by stage, adjacent stage, or valuation tier. Kraken's current listing status and specialized robotics focus within Defense & Security yielded zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-tier private comparables meeting narrow filter criteria. Expanding to all 21 Defense & Security sector companies would introduce material heterogeneity—spanning different subsectors, revenue scales, and market positions—and would not produce a defensible median.

Without a sufficiently tight peer set, this valuation cannot be supported by quantitative triangulation at this time. A credible analysis would require either: (1) expansion of the comp universe to identify at least three true stage-and-tier peers, (2) application of alternative methods (DCF, revenue multiple analysis) supported by internal financial data, or (3) explicit acknowledgment that the public market price, if available, represents the most reliable valuation signal in the absence of comparable-company support.
